name: Interesting Facts
description: A grab bag of broadly-useful general knowledge — geography, history, animals, and language.

questions.q: What is the deepest known point in the Earth's oceans?

source.notes: Deepest point: Challenger Deep, ~10,935 m below sea level.

questions.q: Which national bird represents the United States?

source.notes: Adopted in 1782.

questions.q: Which is the only living structure visible from space?

source.notes: Off the coast of Queensland, Australia. The Great Wall of China is generally NOT visible from low Earth orbit, contrary to common belief.

questions.q: What is the capital of Canada?

source.notes: Capital since 1867.

questions.q: Which Canadian animal is featured on the Canadian nickel?

source.notes: Also a Canadian national symbol.

questions.q: Which Australian marsupial is depicted on the country's coat of arms?

source.notes: Featured alongside the emu — both unable to walk backward, symbolizing forward progress.

questions.q: Which large Hawaiian volcano is the world's most massive active volcano by volume?

source.notes: Mauna Kea is taller (when measured from base); Mauna Loa is more massive.

questions.q: Which Italian city was buried by the eruption of Mt. Vesuvius in 79 CE?

source.notes: Along with Herculaneum and Stabiae.

questions.q: Which Egyptian queen famously had affairs with Julius Caesar and Mark Antony?

source.notes: Cleopatra VII (69–30 BCE), last active ruler of Ptolemaic Egypt.

questions.q: Which Macedonian/Greek conqueror was tutored by Aristotle and conquered most of the known world by age 30?

source.notes: Alexander III of Macedon, 356–323 BCE.

questions.q: Where did Napoleon Bonaparte meet his final defeat in 1815?

source.notes: Defeated by the Duke of Wellington and Marshal Blücher in present-day Belgium.

questions.q: Which Portuguese explorer rounded Africa to reach India by sea in 1498?

source.notes: Reached Calicut on India's southwestern coast.

questions.q: Which 1957 satellite, launched by the Soviet Union, was the first artificial object in space?

source.notes: Launched October 4, 1957. Triggered the Space Race.

questions.q: Which 1919 treaty officially ended World War I?

source.notes: Signed June 28, 1919 — five years to the day after the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand that started the war.

questions.q: What was the first permanent English settlement in North America (founded 1607)?

source.notes: Roanoke (1587) failed mysteriously; Plymouth came later (1620).

questions.q: Which Chinese emperor first unified China and ordered the construction of the Great Wall?

source.notes: Reigned 221–210 BCE. His tomb is famously guarded by the Terracotta Army.

questions.q: Which Swedish inventor of dynamite endowed the Nobel Prizes?

source.notes: Allegedly motivated by reading his own (premature) obituary calling him a 'merchant of death.'

questions.q: What is the longest word in major English-language dictionaries (a lung disease term)?

source.notes: 45 letters; refers to a lung disease caused by inhaling fine silica dust.

questions.q: Which Disney princess was the first of the canon, debuting in 1937?

source.notes: From 'Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs', the first feature-length animated film.

questions.q: South Africa is unusual in having three capitals. Which is the executive (administrative) capital?

source.notes: Cape Town is the legislative capital; Bloemfontein is the judicial capital. Johannesburg is the largest city.
